policy_ai.note: Tier-3: transparent policy AI, submits only through the gate; 5-seed AI-vs-AI campaigns complete all 10 GTs (~100 battles each) and replay byte-exact through verify_game. Declared-weak (no German map-exit play, no Engineer assault stacks, no diversionary attacks, no multi-turn plans). Outcomes are strongly German-favored across seeds: the printed VP schedule (3 VP per Allied LOC failure per turn, 5 VP per Allied unit destroyed vs 1 per German) punishes the simple Allied corridor policy hard; the historical result was also a German win. Both seats run the same policy - beta-fine per the AK/B&G precedent.

grid.provenance: module buildFile HexGrid (dx=96 dy=119 x0=60 y0=60) == the hand-validated legacy values; formula re-validated 2026-07-11 against every printed position in 'Arnhem Historical.vsav' (7 German 18.12 hexes + 3 DZ 18.11 hexes + 8 GT1 18.13 drop targets) - see validate_grid.py

sides.note: detect_tokens generated by make_scenario.py (German counter-image basenames); scenario units carry explicit sides - tokens are the Tier-0/board-layer fallback

stats.provenance: generated by make_scenario.py from counter_stats_by_image.json + the 2026-07-11 full contact-sheet visual pass + the 18.12-18.15 printed schedule (three-source agreement asserted per unit); artillery patterns carry [barrage, defense, MA] - full artillery factors (barrage-FPF-range/def-MA) live on the scenario catalog entries

hexside_rules.cite: [Terrain Key] river hexsides prohibited; bridges and ferries cross (ferry carries its own +3 below); demolition removes 'crossing' [12.13]
[Terrain Key] Ferry +3 MP; ferries are non-bridge hexsides - no ZOC or attacks across [6.33]
[Terrain Key] Stream +3 MP unless bridged; the canals are drawn in stream ink - a demolished canal bridge leaves a stream hexside [12.13]
[5.22] road hex to adjacent road hex through a road hexside = 1/2 MP regardless of other terrain
[5.23] trail hex to adjacent trail hex through a trail hexside = 1 MP regardless of other terrain

zoc.cite: [6.0] all units must cease movement on entering an enemy-controlled hex; [5.14/6.13] a unit may never exit an enemy-controlled hex by movement; [6.11] all units exert ZOC at all times; [6.12] no extra MP; [6.33] ZOC never extends through non-bridge river hexsides (ferries are non-bridge); ZOC does extend across streams

movement.note: stacking is PROHIBITED [5.31] - no stacking spec = units may never end moves on friendlies (engine default); the Engineer 13.24 exception and the free-stacking DZ counters [15.35] are gate-level

classes.note: generated by make_scenario.py from the counter-symbol visual pass; vehicle classes for 5.24 = armor, recon, mech, sp_artillery; airborne flag lives on scenario units (para/glider/ab_artillery/polish)

combat.provenance: Integrated CRT [7.61] transcribed TWO-SOURCE: the printed table's OCR text (Exclusive Rules folder) vs mastermind's independent image transcription (map_tables_transcription.json) - identical on all 12x6 result cells, all terrain-row brackets and both bounds notes; also identical to engine/rules.py (validated 2026-07-03, worked example Town +9 die 5 = D1). validate_combat.py re-runs the cross-check from rules_transcription.json on every run.

crt.alias_cite: [7.61] CRT rows: Rough / Broken-Town-Woods-Stream / Grove-Bridge / Clear-Mixed; [11.2] attacks on City hexes use the Town line

results.Br: Both retreat 1 hex, defender first - a surrounded defender is eliminated [7.62]

crt.bounds_cite: attacks below the lowest differential resolve at the lowest; above +12 as +12 (printed CRT)

artillery.max_per_combat_cite: [14.12] no more than two artillery units per single combat for either Barrage or FPF; no limit on Ground Support Points
artillery.cite: [8.x] barrage within range (target hex counted, firer's not [8.12]); no LOS restrictions [8.61]; adjacent artillery must fight with Barrage strength and suffers results [8.31/8.33] except across rivers [8.34]; pure artillery/GSP attacks: only D2/D3/D4/De apply [8.15]; FPF once per GT [8.46] under the 8.41 conditions

gsp.cite: [9.0/18.16] Allied GSP per the scenario schedule; barrage or FPF, 1 point = 1 strength [9.11]; only within three hexes of an Allied NON-airborne unit [14.11]; never accumulated [9.14]

city.cite: [11.1] units in City hexes reduce remaining retreat results by two (owner's option; D3 min 1, D4 min 2; airborne artillery no reduction); [11.2] negated when surrounded except for Allied airborne/glider units; attacks on cities use the Town CRT line

demolition.cite: [12.x] canal and rail bridges only; German option at the instant the first Allied unit enters an adjacent hex; die 1-2 demolishes; failure = intact forever; highway bridges never; canal bridges Engineer-repairable [12.16/13.1]

exit.cite: [15.4] only German units exit, only during their Movement Phase, paying the exit-terrain cost; exited units return as reinforcements on the same edge segment [15.42]; exit as a combat result = elimination [15.43]

game.title: Arnhem: Operation Market-Garden, September 1944 (Westwall: Four Battles to Germany)
game.art: Redmond A. Simonsen (physical systems and graphics)
game.production: Manfred F. Milkuhn, Larry Catalano, Kevin Zucker, Linda Mosca
game.source: DESIGN CREDITS, Arnhem Exclusive Rules folder (SPI 1976)

module.title: Westwall Quad (VASSAL module)
module.implementation: the module's authors (no credits page found in the module; library listing at vassalengine.org/library, Westwall: Four Battles to Germany)
module.note: Module setup deviations from the printed rules are recorded in rules_transcription.json (module_deviations_worksheet); this scenario follows the printed Historical Scenario [18.1].

credits.note: This engine enforces the printed game's rules against the module's own materials. All game content remains the property of its publisher; module art remains the work of the module authors. Bring-your-own-module: users download the module from the VASSAL module library.

source_defects.note: Defects found in the ORIGINAL PUBLISHED GAME (SPI 1976) during encoding - spec #21. Authority ladder: official errata > tournament/publisher clarification > proven outcome-equivalence > declared umpired.

list.defect: Rule 12.13 says a demolished Canal/Rail bridge hexside 'is treated as if it were a normal Canal or River hexside, subject to all considerations' - but neither the Terrain Key nor the Standard Rules defines a 'Canal' hexside: the Key lists only River (prohibited), Stream (+3), Ferry (+3), Border and the three bridge types, and the CRT has no canal row.
list.resolution: Canals are treated as STREAM hexsides: the map draws all three labeled canals (Wilhelmina, Zuid Willems, Waal-Maas) in the exact stream ink (measured RGB ~(40,160,200) vs river (10,130,172)), the CRT's Broken/Town/Woods/STREAM row covers combat across them, and 5.24 still bars vehicle classes without a bridge - which reproduces the historical Son-bridge situation (infantry wade at +3 MP, armor waits for repair).
list.authority: Map-art evidence + rules-structure inference (no official errata found). Declared interpretation.

list.defect: 15.41 lets German units exit the east side 'between hexes 0126 and 2726, inclusive', but 15.42 says units which exited the east side re-enter 'between hexes 0126 and 0726, inclusive' - the re-entry span is a fraction of the exit span with no rationale, and the two cases are otherwise symmetric (the west side re-uses its exit span verbatim).
list.resolution: Re-entry uses the same span as the exit: 0126-2726. '0726' is read as a typo for '2726'.
list.authority: Internal-consistency inference (west edge is symmetric; no official errata found). Declared interpretation.

list.defect: The Exclusive Rules and the counter sheet disagree on unit designations: the rules' '1/6PT, 2/6PT' and '1/1PT, 2/1PT' print as '1/6P, 2/6P' and '1/1P, 2/1P' on the counters; the rules' 'Hber' group prints as 'Hbr' (combat units) and 'Hber' (artillery); the rules' GT7 Allied glider battalions '1/325, 2/235' include an obvious 2/325 typo.
list.resolution: Counters are matched by designation AND printed factors together (asserted in make_scenario.py); the factor values agree in every case.
list.authority: Counter-sheet evidence (all 95 Arnhem counters read); factors identical under either name.
